FPPC
JUL 16 8 51 AM '90

July 13, 1990
549 lola Ln.
Patterson, Ca 95363

Ms. Peggy Bernardi, Attorney
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J. St. Suite 600
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Dear Ms Bernardi:
Thank you for your consideration and evaluation of the possibility of a conflict of interest problem when a clerical employee of a District Hospital wishes to become a member of the Hospital Board of Directors. Would it be possible to obtain your evaluation in writing?

In discussing your review with Del Puerto Hospital Administrator, Tom Avery, I have learned that the Hospital attorney, Joseph E. Stacls, holds the belief "upon election to the Board of Directors such an employee has a conflict of interest and therefore should not run for election. The conflict would, of course, be removed if she resigns her position as staff nurse upon being elected."

sections.question: Thank you for your consideration and evaluation of the possibility of a conflict of interest problem when a casual employee of a District Hospital wishes to become a member of the Hospital Board of Directors. Would it be possible to obtain your evaluation in writing?
sections.question_synthetic: Does a casual employee of a district hospital have a conflict of interest under California law if they seek election to the hospital's Board of Directors while remaining employed as a staff nurse?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The letter contains the question posed by the requester and references a hospital attorney's legal analysis, but does not include FPPC's written conclusion or advice. Document quality issues with faint handwriting portions noted in original text. No formal conclusion section provided by FPPC.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Thank you for your consideration and evaluation of the possibility of a conflict of interest problem when a casual employee of a District Hospital wishes to become a member of the Hospital Board of Directors. Would it be possible to obtain your evaluation in writing?
embedding.first_500_words: July 13, 1990 547 Lola Ln. Patterson, Ca 95363 Ms. Peggy Bernardi, Attorney Fair Political Practice Commission 428 J. St. Suite 600 Sacramento, Ca 95814 Dear Ms. Bernardi: Thank you for your consideration and evaluation of the possibility of a conflict of interest problem when a casual employee of a District Hospital wishes to become a member of the Hospital Board of Directors. Would it be possible to obtain your evaluation in writing? In discussing your review with Del Puerto Hospital Administrator, Tom Axery, I have learned that the Hospital Attorney, Joseph E. Shanks, holds the belief "upon election to the Board of Directors such an employee has a conflict of interest and therefore should not run for election. The conflict would, of course, be removed if she resigns her position as staff nurse upon being elected." The image provided is extremely faint and mostly illegible, with very little visible text that can be reliably transcribed or read. Only a few scattered letters and numbers are partially visible, but the majority of the content cannot be reliably recovered due to the quality and density of the handwriting. If you have another scan or a higher quality image, please provide it for better results. He lists the underlying goals as "1) Elimination of temptation, 2) avoidance of appearance of impropriety, 3) assurance of the officers undivided and uncompromising allegiance to the Board." He also states: "The most obvious conflict is in the area of personnel and employee relations." He quotes section 1090 in part and interprets its application as such: "In today's environment the Hospital may well have an implied obligation to continue employing the individual in question as a staff nurse. If so, the individual has a direct financial stake in her employment. Because she is in a position to make decisions as to her continued employment as a staff nurse, this individual has the kind of financial stake prohibited by under section 1090." He then states: "So far as we are aware, no court has dealt directly with this situation. But the employee should be aware of the consequences in an adverse decision." He did not deal with the fact that Del Puerto Hospital is a district hospital and, as such, my salary also comes from the district (Stanislaus County). But perhaps, to avoid any problems, it would be simpler to resign my position. Hoping to hear from you. Very truly yours, Betty Carlson The image provided is a handwritten letter, but the handwriting is extremely faint and mostly illegible. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible, such as "1957," "203," and some numbers in the upper right corner. Due to the poor quality and faintness of the ink, it is not possible to transcribe or provide a detailed summary of the content with high confidence. If you have another scan or a higher quality image, please provide it for better results.
embedding.summary: Betty Carlson requests written FPPC evaluation regarding whether a district hospital's casual employee can serve on the Hospital Board of Directors without a conflict of interest, citing the hospital attorney's interpretation of section 1090 and concerns about divided loyalty and financial stake in employment.
